channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated conversation errors for Enterprise and Edu users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Mar 9, 15:56 UTCUpdate - We are experiencing latency on the API and UI endpoints. We are working to resolve the issue.Mar 9, 15:50 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of degraded performance for Webhooks

item.title: Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 42 Deals Announced in February 2026
item.description: Significant cybersecurity M&A deals announced by Check Point, Booz Allen, Proofpoint, Sophos, Palo Alto Networks, and Zscaler. The post Cybersecurity M&A Roundup: 42 Deals Announced in February 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: How AI is turning the Iran conflict into theater
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. “Anyone wanna host a get together in SF and pull this up on a 100 inch TV?”  The author of that post on X was referring to an online intelligence dashboard...

item.title: Codex CLI unresponsive
item.description: Status: InvestigatingWe are investigating the issue for the listed services.Affected components Codex (Degraded performance)

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The activity has been attributed with moderate confidence to the state-sponsored...

item.title: The usability imperative for securing digital asset devices
item.description: When Tony Fadell started working on the iPod, usability often trumped security. The result was an iterative process. Every time someone would find a security weakness or a way to hack the device, the development group would iterate to add measures and fix the issues. Yet, flaws would frequently be...

item.title: The Download: murky AI surveillance laws, and the White House cracks down on defiant labs
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI? The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
item.description: Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me." Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning. That's kind of just how it goes now. The good news? There were some actual wins this week. Real...

item.title: ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection
item.description: Fake CAPTCHA pages instruct victims to paste malicious commands in the Windows Terminal instead of the Run dialog. The post ClickFix Attack Uses Windows Terminal to Evade Detection appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks
item.description: Abusing DNS record management controls, the threat actor hides the location of malicious content via Cloudflare. The post Internet Infrastructure TLD .arpa Abused in Phishing Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partners are defining the security level you must meet. What if you could be the enabler for your...

item.title: Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign
item.description: Threat actors replace legitimate commands on the cloned installation webpages with malicious commands. The post Cloned AI Tool Sites Distribute Malware in ‘InstallFix’ Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
item.description: Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code, and harvest sensitive data. The extensions in question, both originally associated with a developer...

item.title: Former vivo Star Product Manager Song Ziwei Launches AI Hardware Startup, Raises Over RMB 100 Million
item.description: Former vivo star product manager Song Ziwei has raised over $14 million for her AI hardware startup building a fashion-focused smart mirror powered by an AI agent.

item.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
item.description: High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign. The activity, which has targeted aviation, energy, government, law enforcement, pharmaceutical, technology, and telecommunications sectors, has been...

item.title: Xiaohongshu Releases FireRed-Image-Edit 1.1 Model for Advanced Image Editing
item.description: Xiaohongshu has released FireRed-Image-Edit 1.1, an image-editing model focused on identity consistency and high-efficiency generation, capable of producing results in 4.5 seconds with 30GB VRAM.

item.title: Former Xiaomi Executive Denies Rumors of Launching Automotive Solar Startup
item.description: Former Xiaomi executive Li Chuangqi dismissed rumors that he launched an automotive solar startup with Xiaomi, calling the reports “false.”

item.title: Simplexity Robotics Raises RMB 2 Billion in Just Six Months, Becoming the Fastest “Unicorn” in Embodied AI
item.description: Chinese embodied AI startup Simplexity Robotics has raised $280 million in under six months, becoming the fastest unicorn in the sector with backing from Tencent and Alibaba.

item.title: RoboLayout: Differentiable 3D Scene Generation for Embodied Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.05522v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in vision language models (VLMs) have shown strong potential for spatial reasoning and 3D scene layout generation from open-ended language instructions. However, generating layouts that are not only semantically coherent but also...

item.title: Real-Time AI Service Economy: A Framework for Agentic Computing Across the Continuum
item.description: arXiv:2603.05614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-time AI services increasingly operate across the device-edge-cloud continuum, where autonomous AI agents generate latency-sensitive workloads, orchestrate multi-stage processing pipelines, and compete for shared resources under policy and...

item.title: Reasoning Models Struggle to Control their Chains of Thought
item.description: arXiv:2603.05706v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring is a promising tool for detecting misbehaviors and understanding the motivations of modern reasoning models. However, if models can control what they verbalize in their CoT, it could undermine CoT monitorability. To...

item.title: Evolving Medical Imaging Agents via Experience-driven Self-skill Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2603.05860v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical image interpretation is inherently multi-step and tool-centric: clinicians iteratively combine visual evidence with patient context, quantify findings, and refine their decisions through a sequence of specialized procedures. While LLM-based...

item.title: The World Won't Stay Still: Programmable Evolution for Agent Benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2603.05910v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-powered agents fulfill user requests by interacting with environments, querying data, and invoking tools in a multi-turn process. Yet, most existing benchmarks assume static environments with fixed schemas and toolsets, neglecting the evolutionary...

item.title: Traversal-as-Policy: Log-Distilled Gated Behavior Trees as Externalized, Verifiable Policies for Safe, Robust, and Efficient Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.05517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous LLM agents fail because long-horizon policy remains implicit in model weights and transcripts, while safety is retrofitted post hoc. We propose Traversal-as-Policy: distill sandboxed OpenHands execution logs into a single executable Gated...

item.title: JAWS: Enhancing Long-term Rollout of Neural Operators via Spatially-Adaptive Jacobian Regularization
item.description: arXiv:2603.05538v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Data-driven surrogate models improve the efficiency of simulating continuous dynamical systems, yet their autoregressive rollouts are often limited by instability and spectral blow-up. While global regularization techniques can enforce contractive...

item.title: VDCook:DIY video data cook your MLLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.05539v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce VDCook: a self-evolving video data operating system, a configurable video data construction platform for researchers and vertical domain teams. Users initiate data requests via natural language queries and adjustable parameters (scale,...

item.title: IntSeqBERT: Learning Arithmetic Structure in OEIS via Modulo-Spectrum Embeddings
item.description: arXiv:2603.05556v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integer sequences in the OEIS span values from single-digit constants to astronomical factorials and exponentials, making prediction challenging for standard tokenised models that cannot handle out-of-vocabulary values or exploit periodic arithmetic...

item.title: Autocorrelation effects in a stochastic-process model for decision making via time series
item.description: arXiv:2603.05559v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Decision makers exploiting photonic chaotic dynamics obtained by semiconductor lasers provide an ultrafast approach to solving multi-armed bandit problems by using a temporal optical signal as the driving source for sequential decisions. In such...

item.title: Verify as You Go: An LLM-Powered Browser Extension for Fake News Detection
item.description: arXiv:2603.05519v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rampant spread of fake news in the digital age poses serious risks to public trust and democratic institutions, underscoring the need for effective, transparent, and user-centered detection tools. Existing browser extensions often fall short due...

item.title: Attention Meets Reachability: Structural Equivalence and Efficiency in Grammar-Constrained LLM Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2603.05540v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study grammar-constrained decoding (GCD) as a coupling between an autoregressive next-token distribution and a reachability oracle over a pushdown system compiled from a context-free grammar (CFG). We prove an oracle invariance theorem:...

item.title: NOTAI.AI: Explainable Detection of Machine-Generated Text via Curvature and Feature Attribution
item.description: arXiv:2603.05617v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present NOTAI.AI, an explainable framework for machine-generated text detection that extends Fast-DetectGPT by integrating curvature-based signals with neural and stylometric features in a supervised setting. The system combines 17 interpretable...

item.title: Safer Reasoning Traces: Measuring and Mitigating Chain-of-Thought Leakage in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.05618v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting improves LLM reasoning but can increase privacy risk by resurfacing personally identifiable information (PII) from the prompt into reasoning traces and outputs, even under policies that instruct the model not to...

item.title: The Fragility Of Moral Judgment In Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.05651v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: People increasingly use large language models (LLMs) for everyday moral and interpersonal guidance, yet these systems cannot interrogate missing context and judge dilemmas as presented. We introduce a perturbation framework for testing the stability...

item.title: Incident with Codespaces
item.description: Mar 9, 03:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Mar 9, 03:51 UTCUpdate - This incident has been resolved. New Codespace creation requests...

item.title: OpenClaw Mania Sweeps AI World as Chinese Tech Giants Race for the Next AI Gateway
item.description: OpenClaw’s explosive rise—now the most-starred project on GitHub—has triggered a scramble among Chinese tech giants like Xiaomi and Tencent to capture the next major AI agent gateway.

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these...

item.title: Recent Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Vulnerability Now Widely Exploited
item.description: WatchTowr reports seeing exploitation attempts for CVE-2026-20127 from numerous unique IP addresses. The post Recent Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Vulnerability Now Widely Exploited appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: AWS - Europe (Zürich): INC0153183
item.description: Mar 7, 20:05 UTCInvestigating - Current status: We're investigating an issue with Snowflake Data Cloud. We'll provide an update within 60 minutes.Customer experience: Customers hosted in the specified regions may be unable to access or use multiple core Snowflake services and features. Affected...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Mar 7, 13:58 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: [AINews] AI Engineer will be the LAST job
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on the jobs debate.

item.title: Login Issues
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Login (Operational) Login (Operational) Login (Operational)

item.title: Errors with Compliance API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Compliance API (Operational)

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Mar 6, 20:18 UTCUpdate - We have deployed a fix for the issue causing some users to experience intermittent failures when accessing the Webhooks API and configuration pages. We are monitoring to confirm full recovery.Mar 6, 19:39 UTCUpdate - We continue working on mitigations to restore service.Mar...

item.title: Access Configuration Updates Delayed
item.description: Mar 6, 20:06 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 6, 19:30 UTCInvestigating - Access configuration changes are experiencing delays propagating to the edge. Existing configurations and logins are not impacted.

item.title: Is the Pentagon allowed to surveil Americans with AI?
item.description: The ongoing public feud between the Department of Defense and the AI company Anthropic has raised a deep and still unanswered question: Does the law actually allow the US government to conduct mass surveillance on Americans? Surprisingly, the answer is not straightforward. More than a decade after...

item.title: Issues with Increased Codex Usage Rate
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified an issue causing Codex usage to be consumed faster than expected, which may cause some users to hit rate limits sooner than intended. We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components Codex (Degraded performance)

item.title: Conversational LLM Evaluations in Minutes with NVIDIA NeMo Evaluator Agent Skills
Filesystem connector missing from Claude Desktop
item.description: Mar 6, 17:16 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix has been implemented. Users may need to re-add the Filesystem connector to their organization allowlist in order to reactivate it.

item.title: Intermittent connectivity issues in Ashburn, VA
item.description: Mar 6, 16:31 UTCMonitoring - Cloudflare investigated intermittent connectivity issues in Ashburn, VA. A fix for this issue was implemented and we are currently monitoring the results. We will update the status once the issue is resolved.
